title: rubus serpens var. racemulosus
intro: Rubus serpens var. racemulosus, also known as the creeping blackberry or thimbleberry, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Rosaceae family. It is a medicinal plant with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, traditionally used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and digestive problems. The plant's leaves, stems, and roots have been used in herbal preparations to provide relief from pain, reduce fever, and promote overall health and well-being. Rubus serpens var. racemulosus is often used in herbal teas, salves, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
